content.boundary: Applies nationally to all decisions concerning village primary schools under 2,000-inhabitant settlements; the registry and transparency rules are advisory/informational and do not themselves mandate closure, retention, or any specific structural model.
content.change_lever: Creation of a permanent, independent evidence and transparency body maintaining (a) standardized minimum-viability threshold definitions, (b) mandatory publication of true per-pupil costs including transportation offsets, (c) teacher-training/multigrade pedagogy standards, and (d) a longitudinal closure/outcome registry tracking segregation, achievement, and demographic indicators post-decision — functioning as an information layer rather than a decision-making authority.
content.name: National evidence and transparency infrastructure: viability registry, cost-visibility rules, and no-new-policy baseline
content.system_problem: Hungary currently lacks any standing, institutionalized evidence infrastructure for school-network decisions: closure and retention cycles have historically been driven by top-down political decisions rather than demographic-trigger rules or transparent cost data, cost-saving claims from consolidation are asserted but rarely verified, and no closure/outcome registry exists to track downstream effects (segregation, achievement, community, demographic) of past decisions, meaning each new proposal (T1-T5) is being designed without a shared evidentiary base or the ability to learn from prior outcomes.
